role_title: Senior Associate, Strategy & Operations - Commerce Platform
secondary_archetype: Growth PM (adjacent — go-to-market and product roadmap influence)

level_signals: 3+ years of experience in consulting, strategy, or operations
Direct ownership of top priority projects and merchant relationships

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have a Bachelor's degree or higher
You have 3+ years of experience in consulting, strategy, or operations; high-growth/early-stage startup or entrepreneurial experience is a big plus
Your expert communication and interpersonal skills set you apart
You are a strong analytical thinker who can provide applicable recommendations out of a complex, sometimes murky, dataset
You have a bias towards action and are able to thrive in a fast-paced, constantly changing work environment
Your charisma allows you to excel at connecting with Merchants and internal teams to promote products and drive adoption
You crave responsibility and want to shape the vision and direction of the company
You're willing to get your hands dirty and you're open to feedback.
You're excellent at motivating potential partners to see the benefits DoorDash Commerce Platform will bring to their business

Must be comfortable regularly exercising discretion and independent judgment in performing job duties, including evaluating options, making informed decisions, and determining appropriate courses of action within the scope of assigned responsibilities.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: high-growth/early-stage startup or entrepreneurial experience is a big plus

scope.surface: DoorDash Commerce Platform — ecommerce fulfillment for restaurants, grocers, and retailers' first-party experiences
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / platform — enabling merchant partners (restaurants, grocers, retailers) to power their own ecommerce fulfillment
primary_metrics_inferred: Growth and adoption of Commerce Platform products
Go-to-market conversion and scaling of partnerships
core_responsibilities: Drive growth and adoption of top priority Commerce Platform projects
Develop and foster relationships with top priority merchant partners
Build go-to-market strategy including product market fit, pricing, and competitive landscape analysis
Guide operational improvements for merchant partners and steer product roadmap via quantitative analysis and qualitative feedback
Work cross-functionally with Product, Engineering, Finance, Analytics, Support, Sales, and Account Management
Evaluate and test various revenue, cost, and quality drivers

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly stated ($87K–$128K base nationally)
Equity grants mentioned as part of compensation
Comprehensive benefits package including 401(k) with employer matching, 16 weeks paid parental leave, wellness benefits
Clear product surface and ownership scope described
Applications accepted on an ongoing basis — no hard deadline pressure
High-growth platform with broad merchant types (restaurants, grocers, retailers)
red_flags: Location/work modality is entirely unclear — no city, remote, or hybrid policy stated in JD body; NYC Covey notice implies possible NYC office tie but not definitive
Role spans multiple functions (BD, product ops, data analysis, go-to-market) which could indicate diffuse scope or understaffing
Salary range is relatively modest ($87K–$128K) given seniority expectations and cost of living in likely markets

fields_inferred_not_stated: location.type (not stated; marked 'unclear' — NYC Covey notice suggests possible NYC association but body does not confirm)
seniority.level (inferred from title 'Senior Associate', years requirement, and comp range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not in JD)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from statement 'Base salary is localized according to an employee's work location')
ambiguities: Location/work modality is never specified in the JD body. The presence of a NYC Covey AEDT notice implies a possible NYC office connection, but the role could also be remote or in another city. Marked 'unclear'.

The 3+ years requirement mentions 'consulting, strategy, or operations' as the primary path, with startup experience as 'a big plus' — the startup experience bullet is placed within the same sentence as the hard requirement, making the boundary between must-have and nice-to-have slightly ambiguous. Treated startup experience as nice-to-have.
